title: Getting started with edr4r
text: client just stores connection settings (base URL, user agent, timeout, retry policy). Pass verbose = TRUE echo every request URL, headers = attach auth tokens.
code: library(edr4r) library(ggplot2)  client <- edr_client("https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app") client #> <edr_client> #>   base_url:   <https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app> #>   user_agent: edr4r/0.1.0 (+https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r) #>   timeout:    60s #>   max_tries:  3

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: edr_collections() lists every EDR collection service serves. data_queries column tells EDR query types collection supports (locations, cube, area, …). Hit verb server doesn’t implement get HTTP error. see data parameters collection exposes (values can pass parameter_name = query verbs), use edr_parameters(): edr_queryables() something different – returns OGC queryables JSON Schema (filter properties CQL2 / OGC API Features). discovering parameter names, edr_parameters() want.

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: collections support cube, edr_explore() fetches every station’s data one bulk request renders map per-station popups – much faster fetching one station time. rise-edr advertises cube, default method = "auto" takes path. object prints interactive leaflet map R session; blue marker opens larger plot/CSV popup station. Markers data blue (clickable popup plot + CSV); stations bbox covers returned data requested parameter/window dimmed grey. legend bottom-right explains distinction. Save map standalone HTML (selfcontained, sidecar directory):
code: m <- edr_explore(   client, "rise-edr",   bbox           = c(-114, 32, -111, 38),         # SW US   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-06-30",   parameter_name = "3",   popup          = "plot+csv",   label_col      = "locationName",   quiet          = TRUE ) edr_save_html(m, "wwdh-southwest.html")

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: datetime forgiving: pass "start/end", open interval like "2020-01-01/..", length-2 character vector. gets normalised ISO-8601 form server expects. parameter_name character vector. ’s sent one comma-separated parameter-name query parameter. monitoring networks use compound station IDs (colon-separated triplets like "1185:CO:SNTL" show snow forecast networks). work -– reserved characters get URL-encoded . literal / ID rejected, can’t survive round trip HTTP path segments matter encode . every server implements every EDR verb. locations, position, cube, area common; radius, trajectory, corridor less . client supports per spec, call collection doesn’t implement given verb returns HTTP error.

title: USGS streamgages: discover and map a basin
text: USGS quirk flag front: ask specific station edr_location(), server ignores datetime interval just returns recent limit records (limit defaults 10). get five months daily data, pass limit comfortably covers window – say 200 days – filter target window client-side. edr_plot() small ggplot wrapper tidy tibble: plot chunk plot-one-gauge
code: example_id   <- gauges$id[[1]] example_name <- gauges$monitoring_location_name[[1]]  resp <- edr_location(   usgs, "daily-edr",   location_id    = example_id,   parameter_name = "00060",   limit          = 200 ) df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp)  # Filter to the Jan–May 2026 window we care about df_jan_may <- df[df$datetime >= as.POSIXct("2026-01-01", tz = "UTC") &                  df$datetime <= as.POSIXct("2026-05-31", tz = "UTC"), ] head(df_jan_may[, c("datetime", "value", "unit")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   datetime            value unit  #>   <dttm>              <dbl> <chr> #> 1 2026-05-31 00:00:00  2.21 ft3/s #> 2 2026-05-30 00:00:00  2.53 ft3/s #> 3 2026-05-29 00:00:00  2.66 ft3/s #> 4 2026-05-28 00:00:00  3.14 ft3/s #> 5 2026-05-27 00:00:00  3.13 ft3/s #> 6 2026-05-26 00:00:00  3.29 ft3/s edr_plot(df_jan_may) +   ggtitle(paste0(example_name, " — daily streamflow"))

what: 4. Map every gauge with per-station popups
title: USGS streamgages: discover and map a basin
text: edr_explore() per-station loop hands results edr_map() one call. marker becomes inline plot + CSV download gauge. Click blue marker open popup. Jan–May 2026 streamflow plot inline; “Download CSV” link delivers gauge’s series. save map standalone HTML file:
code: m <- edr_explore(   usgs, "daily-edr",   bbox           = triangle_bbox,   parameter_name = "00060",   record_limit   = 200,                 # ~6 months of daily values per station   popup          = "plot+csv",   label_col      = "monitoring_location_name",   quiet          = TRUE ) # Embed the interactive map inline via a base64 data URI so the vignette # is fully selfcontained (no sidecar files in inst/doc/). tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") edr_save_html(m, tmp) data_uri <- paste0(   "data:text/html;charset=utf-8;base64,",   base64enc::base64encode(tmp) ) unlink(tmp) cat(sprintf(   '<iframe src="%s" width="100%%" height="520" style="border:1px solid #ddd;"></iframe>',   data_uri )) edr_save_html(m, "triangle-streamgages.html")

what: A few things worth knowing about the USGS endpoint
title: USGS streamgages: discover and map a basin
text: daily-edr advertises locations query. EDR verbs (cube, area, position, …) return HTTP errors. per-station fallback works fine tens gauges; hundreds consider pre-filtering aggressively bbox =. datetime honoured /locations/{id} requests – server returns latest limit records regardless. Pass enough limit cover window, filter client-side. station id format "USGS-<NWIS-id>". bare NWIS id ("02087500") doesn’t work; pass full "USGS-..." form, edr_locations() returns id column. 44-column attribute table gauges carries lot useful metadata (drainage_area, state_name, hydrologic_unit_code, altitude, …). Use filter label mapping. Pass label_col = "monitoring_location_name" edr_map() popup titles read “BUFFALO CREEK NEAR FALLS, NC” instead raw USGS-02087183 id.

title: Western Water Datahub collection field guide
text: EDR service layers: service landing page tells top-level resources exist. /collections lists datasets, called collections. /collections/{id} describes one collection, including parameters, extent, links, supported EDR query types. Query endpoints retrieve either station/location metadata, feature geometries, sampled environmental values. practice, WWDH collections fall three broad families. important distinction : parameter_names data variables can ask , reservoir storage, SWE, stage, precipitation, maximum temperature. edr4r, discover edr_parameters() pass query helpers parameter_name =. queryables filterable feature properties, id, name, valid_time, basin_index. Discover edr_queryables(). useful /items collections feature-style filtering.

title: Western Water Datahub collection field guide
text: Start every WWDH session listing collections advertised query types. data_queries column route map. contains locations, collection site index. contains cube, can usually pull many sites grid cells inside bbox one request. contains area, can query polygon. empty, assume collection useless; often OGC API Features layer, use edr_queryables() edr_items(). inspect one collection retrieving data: metadata browsable without R: https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app/collections?f=html https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app/collections/snotel-edr?f=html https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app/collections/snotel-edr/queryables?f=html

title: Western Water Datahub collection field guide
text: Station-style collections two parts: location index sampled values. Start small bbox one parameter. providers honor limit /locations, return matching sites inside spatial filter. normal provider variation. Keep bbox small exploring. one station, prefer edr_cube() collection advertises . one server request across bbox, rather one request per station. long table returned covjson_to_tibble() core columns across station, grid, profile data: Reservoir-oriented collections follow pattern. useful parameter examples: fast exploratory products, edr_explore() chooses bulk query possible returns either leaflet map, ggplot, tidy data.

title: Western Water Datahub collection field guide
text: WWDH collections advertise EDR data queries. usually feature layers: polygons, lines, points attributes. still matter water analysis can carry forecast products, drought categories, basin summaries, boundaries, current status attributes. workflow : catalog layers, treat /items feature download, time-series sampling query. layer large provider-backed, begin small bbox low limit. upstream wrappers sensitive particular filter combinations, request fails, try order: Open HTML collection page ?f=html. Open queryables page check exact field names. Try /items?f=json&limit=1. Try /items?f=json&bbox=minx,miny,maxx,maxy. Remove optional filters add back one time.
code: q <- edr_queryables(wwdh, "snotel-huc06-means") names(q$properties) #> [1] "geometry" #> [2] "name" #> [3] "geoconnex_url" #> [4] "id" #> [5] "number_of_stations_used_for_basin_index_calculation" #> [6] "current_snow_water_equivalent_relative_to_thirty_year_avg" #> [7] "basin_index"  huc_swe <- edr_items(wwdh, "snotel-huc06-means", limit = 3) names(huc_swe) #>  [1] "id" #>  [2] "name" #>  [3] "station_list" #>  [4] "current_snow_water_equivalent_relative_to_thirty_year_avg" #>  [5] "basin_index" #>  [6] "median_values" #>  [7] "latest_values" #>  [8] "number_of_stations_used_for_basin_index_calculation" #>  [9] "geoconnex_url" #> [10] "latest_full_day_of_data" #> [11] "geometry"

text: WWDH request fails, usually one : Good habits: Start one parameter one short time window. Use bbox even endpoint can technically return everything. Prefer cube bulk station/grid retrieval advertised. Prefer /items feature/catalog layers EDR data queries. Save intermediate results CSV RDS query trust, rather re-hitting live service every analysis step.

title: Western Water Datahub collection field guide
text: works, enlarge one dimension time: wider bbox, longer date range, parameters. makes failures easier diagnose keeps shared API responsive.
code: library(edr4r)  wwdh <- edr_client("https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app", verbose = TRUE)  # 1. Pick a collection. collections <- edr_collections(wwdh) collections[, c("id", "title", "data_queries")]  # 2. Inspect variables and feature filters. edr_parameters(wwdh, "rise-edr") edr_queryables(wwdh, "rise-edr")  # 3. Find sites or features. locs <- edr_locations(   wwdh, "rise-edr",   bbox  = c(-116, 35, -114, 37),   limit = 25 )  # 4. Pull a small data sample. resp <- edr_cube(   wwdh, "rise-edr",   bbox           = c(-116, 35, -114, 37),   datetime       = "2024-01-01/2024-03-31",   parameter_name = "3" )  df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp)  # 5. Visualize or export. edr_plot(df) edr_map(locs, data = df, popup = "plot+csv") write.csv(df, "wwdh-sample.csv", row.names = FALSE)

what: 5b. The items catalog: filter, browse, download

what: 7. Get data for many locations at once: /cube

title: Tutorial: USBR RISE data through the Western Water Datahub EDR API
text: Start small — one location, one parameter, one month. Widen one dimension time small request works.
code: 1. ORIENT      /collections/rise-edr                  (browser, f=html) 2. PARAMETERS  /collections/rise-edr?f=json           -> parameter_names: pick exact ids 3. LOCATIONS   /collections/rise-edr/items?f=html&bbox=...&{queryable}=...&limit=...                -> browse table; switch to f=csv to download; note location ids 4. ONE SERIES  /collections/rise-edr/locations/{id}?f=json&datetime=...&parameter-name=... 5. MANY SERIES /collections/rise-edr/cube?f=json&bbox=...&datetime=...&parameter-name=... 6. CROSSWALK   /collections?f=json -> parameterGroups, then repeat 3-5 on the sibling collection

what: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: R client service speaks OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR). spec general, practice package gets use -situ monitoring networks — stream gauges, weather stations, snow telemetry, reservoir telemetry — expose stations time series EDR. Two known-good places point : USGS waterdata OGC API — stream gauges water-quality stations U.S. Geological Survey. Western Water Datahub — pygeoapi deployment wraps RISE, SNOTEL, USACE, AWDB monitoring sources behind single EDR endpoint. goal take tedious parts EDR hands — URL construction, comma-separated parameter lists, WKT coordinate encoding, retries, content negotiation — hand back something can actually data analysis : CoverageJSON → long tibble (one row per coverage × parameter × time step), via covjson_to_tibble(). GeoJSON → sf object, via geojson_to_sf().

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: local development: Requires R >= 4.1. sf package optional recommended (used turn location lists GeoJSON spatial objects).
code: # from GitHub (recommended) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("ksonda/edr4r")  # or # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ksonda/edr4r") git clone https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r.git cd edr4r R -e 'devtools::install()'

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Start pointing client server. base URL thing really needs: collection IDs (monitoring-locations, daily-values) ones used placeholders — every server advertises . first thing new service run edr_collections() read data_queries column see EDR endpoints collection supports.
code: library(edr4r)  client <- edr_client("https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta") # or "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app" # or "http://localhost:5005" if you're running pygeoapi locally  edr_collections(client) #> # A tibble: N × 7 #>   id                   title                description  extent_bbox crs   data_queries links #>   <chr>                <chr>                <chr>        <list>      <chr> <list>       <list> #> 1 monitoring-locations Monitoring locations ...          <dbl [4]>   ...   <chr [3]>    ... #> 2 daily-values         Daily values         ...          <dbl [4]>   ...   <chr [3]>    ... #> ...

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: edr_locations() filters returns full station list GeoJSON. sf installed, gets promoted sf object automatically:
code: locs <- edr_locations(client, "monitoring-locations") locs                            # sf POINTs with station attributes plot(sf::st_geometry(locs))

what: Pull a time series for one station
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: know station ID, ask values. server returns CoverageJSON; covjson_to_tibble() flattens one row per (coverage × parameter × timestamp):
code: resp <- edr_location(   client, "daily-values",   location_id    = "08313000",   datetime       = "2020-01-01/2020-12-31",   parameter_name = c("discharge", "gage_height") )  df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp) df #> # A tibble: 732 × 9 #>   coverage_id parameter   parameter_label  unit  datetime                x     y     z value #>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>            <chr> <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 08313000    discharge   Discharge        ft3/s 2020-01-01 00:00:00 -109.  37.0    NA   240 #> ...

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: grab everything inside rectangle, use edr_cube(): arbitrary polygon, edr_area() takes WKT, sf polygon, matrix (lon, lat) rows (’ll close ring ):
code: cube <- edr_cube(   client, "daily-values",   bbox           = c(-120, 39, -118, 41),   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-03-31",   parameter_name = "discharge" ) covjson_to_tibble(cube) ring <- matrix(   c(-109, 47, -104, 47, -104, 49, -109, 49),   ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE ) area <- edr_area(client, "monitoring-locations", coords = ring,                  datetime = "2022-01-01/..") covjson_to_tibble(area)

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: edr_plot() small ggplot2 wrapper tidy tibble: Facets parameter (different units don’t share y-axis) colours station. Add layers themes like ggplot. also auto-detects common non-station shapes:
code: edr_plot(resp)            # accepts an edr_response directly edr_plot(cube)            # x/y grid -> tile map edr_plot(profile)         # varying z -> vertical profile  # or force the layout edr_plot(profile, view = "profile") edr_plot(cube, view = "grid")

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: edr_map() puts stations leaflet basemap. Pass data = named list keyed station id (shape [edr_explore()] produces) marker gets popup inline plot “Download CSV” link station’s data — embedded data: URI saved HTML selfcontained: quick exploratory pass whole collection, edr_explore() fetch + plot + map one call: Gridded coverages vertical profiles can mapped . edr_map() detects tidy CoverageJSON grids/profiles puts slice selectors inside leaflet widget multiple parameters datetimes; grids also get z selector multiple vertical levels present: edr_explore() uses behavior bulk coverage queries. Use output = "plot" want ggplot instead interactive map:
code: stations <- edr_locations(client, "monitoring-locations",                           bbox = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5)) data_list <- list("3514" = covjson_to_tibble(resp)) m <- edr_map(stations, data = data_list, popup = "plot+csv") edr_save_html(m, "stations.html") edr_explore(   client, "daily-values",   bbox           = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5),   datetime       = "2024-01-01/2024-03-31",   parameter_name = "discharge",   limit          = 25,   file           = "snapshot.html" ) grid <- covjson_to_tibble(cube) edr_map(grid)  profile <- covjson_to_tibble(profile_resp) edr_map(profile) edr_explore(client, "gridded-collection",             bbox = c(-120, 39, -118, 41),             method = "cube")  edr_explore(client, "profile-collection",             coords = c(-119, 40),             method = "position")  edr_explore(client, "profile-collection",             coords = c(-119, 40),             method = "position", output = "plot")

what: Weird IDs, CSV, and an escape hatch
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: monitoring networks use compound station IDs — colon-separated triplets common pattern. client URL-encodes reserved characters : server advertises CSV, can ask instead CovJSON: need hit endpoint package doesn’t wrap (instances, custom queryables, anything weird), edr_request() raw escape hatch:
code: edr_location(client, "station-network", "1185:CO:SNTL",              datetime = "2024-01-01/..") edr_location(client, "daily-values", "08313000",              datetime = "2010-01-01/..", format = "csv") edr_request(client, "collections/daily-values/instances", format = "json")

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: server actually supports varies. Every query verb EDR spec supported client, servers implement subset. -situ monitoring deployments, locations, position, cube, area common; radius, trajectory, corridor less . Hitting verb server doesn’t implement gives HTTP error. Check data_queries column edr_collections() assume query work.

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Every query verb accepts standard EDR filters: datetime — ISO-8601 instant interval. Accepts "2020-01-01/2020-12-31", open interval "2020-01-01/..", length-2 character vector c("2020-01-01", "2020-12-31"). parameter_name — character vector parameter names; sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. Use edr_parameters() discover valid names. bbox — numeric length-4 (minx, miny, maxx, maxy) length-6 (z). coords — position/area/radius/trajectory/corridor: WKT string, numeric vector / 2-column matrix lon-lat, sf/sfc geometry. z, crs, limit — passed supplied. ... — extra query parameter forwarded verbatim.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: Flattens CoverageJSON Coverage CoverageCollection long tibble one row per (coverage, parameter, time-step). Handles Point PointSeries domain types used station-based EDR providers, falls back general N-dimensional unrolling Grid-like domains.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
code: covjson_to_tibble(x, datetime_as_posix = TRUE)

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: x CoverageJSON object: either edr_response returned edr_location() / edr_area() / edr_cube() (etc.) format = "covjson", raw parsed list. datetime_as_posix TRUE (default), attempts parse time axis POSIXct (UTC). Falls back character failure.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: tibble columns coverage_id, parameter, parameter_label, unit, datetime, x, y, z, value. Columns absent source filled NA.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: base_url Base URL OGC API - EDR service. Examples: USGS waterdata OGC API "https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta", Western Water Datahub "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app", "http://localhost:5005" local pygeoapi dev server. trailing slash optional. user_agent String sent User-Agent header. Defaults "edr4r/<version> (+https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r)". timeout Request timeout seconds. Defaults 60. max_tries Maximum number attempts per request. client retries 408, 429, 5xx responses exponential backoff. Defaults 3. headers Named character vector extra headers attached every request (e.g. c(Authorization = "Bearer ...")). verbose TRUE, prints request URLs console made. Useful debugging.

text: object class edr_client.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. bbox Optional numeric length-4 bbox. Used filter locations index (server honours ) bbox cube fetch method = "auto". omitted method = "cube", derived bounding box returned locations sf. coords Point coords position, polygon coords area. Forwarded edr_position() / edr_area(). datetime ISO-8601 interval forwarded data fetch. parameter_name Character vector parameter ids; forwarded data fetch. Use edr_parameters() discover valid ids. limit Optional cap number stations map. record_limit Optional per-station record cap, passed edr_location() per-location path. Useful servers (e.g. USGS waterdata) cap responses ~10 records default. Ignored cube area paths. file non-NULL, write map HTML path via edr_save_html() return file invisibly. Otherwise return leaflet map. popup Popup mode (forwarded edr_map()). method One "auto" (default), "cube", "area", "position", "per-location". See . output One "auto" (default), "map", "plot", "data". "auto" returns station map station time-series results interactive coverage map gridded/profile results. plot_view Plot view passed edr_plot() returning plot. Defaults "auto". quiet FALSE (default), print cli progress bar falling back per-location fetches. ... Forwarded edr_map() returning map.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: leaflet htmlwidget, ggplot, tidy tibble/list output = "data", invisible(file) map saved.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: default method = "auto" picks cheapest route collection advertises data_queries: cube – one HTTP call returning CoverageCollection across whole bbox. Fast. Used collection supports cube bbox supplied. area – like cube uses polygon. Used coords supplied collection supports area. position – one HTTP call point. Useful vertical profiles returned position queries. per-location – fallback: one HTTP call per station via edr_location(). Slower (N+1), used neither spatial bulk query supported matching spatial input supplied. Force specific path setting method. coords required area position; method = "cube" bbox omitted, bbox derived returned locations.

"auto" (default) uses station markers spatial feature inputs, grid cells gridded coverage data, profile markers vertical profiles. title: edr4r (development version)
text: Preserve mixed numeric/text CoverageJSON values character instead silently converting text values NA. Make edr_explore(method = "auto") choose bulk spatial queries matching spatial input supplied. Add grid vertical-profile plot views edr_plot(), automatic view detection. Allow edr_explore() return plots data gridded/profile coverage queries, including method = "position" profile-style responses. Add interactive coverage maps edr_map() edr_explore(), -map selectors gridded coverage slices (parameter, datetime, z) profile selectors parameter / datetime. Materialize CoverageJSON regular-grid axes declared start, stop, num, used WWDH usgs-prism collection. Warn per-station edr_explore() fallback fetches fail. Tighten collection-id, coordinate, WKT validation. Add max_match_distance edr_map() bounded spatial matching.

title: edr4r 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Discovery: edr_landing(), edr_conformance(), edr_collections(), edr_collection(), edr_queryables(). Query verbs: edr_locations() / edr_location(), edr_items() / edr_item(), edr_position(), edr_area(), edr_cube(), edr_radius(), edr_trajectory(), edr_corridor(). Low-level escape hatch: edr_request(). Parsers: covjson_to_tibble() CoverageJSON, geojson_to_sf() GeoJSON.
